choices.explanation: chronic atrophic gastritis from pernicious anaemia → achlorhydria → bacterial overgrowth → N-nitroso compound formation → gastric intestinal metaplasia → gastric adenocarcinoma; 2–3× increased risk

questions.stem: Unprotected sexual intercourse with several sexual partners.

choices.label: J
choices.explanation: HPV (types 16, 18) transmitted sexually → oncoproteins E6 (degrades p53) + E7 (degrades Rb) → uncontrolled cell cycle; cervical screening (smear/colposcopy) + HPV vaccination for primary prevention

questions.stem: A 66-year-old woman treated with an alkylating agent for myeloma presents with severe haematuria.

choices.label: M
choices.explanation: alkylating agent; metabolite acrolein is directly urotoxic → haemorrhagic cystitis; prevention = mesna (uroprotectant) + aggressive IV hydration; chlorambucil is also an alkylating agent but haemorrhagic cystitis is specifically associated with cyclophospha…

questions.keyPoint: alkylating agent; metabolite acrolein is directly urotoxic → haemorrhagic cystitis; prevention = mesna (uroprotectant) + aggressive IV hydration; chlorambucil is also an alkylating agent but haemorrhagic cystitis is specifically associated with cyclophosphamide/ifosfamide

questions.stem: A 34-year-old woman treated with a microtubule-inhibitor for carcinoma of the breast develops a peripheral neuropathy.

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: taxane = microtubule stabiliser (prevents depolymerisation); neurotoxicity via disruption of axonal microtubule transport → sensorimotor peripheral neuropathy; vinca alkaloids (vincristine) also cause neuropathy but are microtubule destabilisers

questions.stem: A young man treated with an antibiotic for acute leukaemia develops arrhythmias and heart block shortly after the administration of chemotherapy.

choices.label: J
choices.explanation: anthracycline antibiotic; acute cardiotoxicity = arrhythmias + heart block immediately post-infusion; chronic cardiotoxicity = dilated cardiomyopathy (cumulative dose-dependent, limit to 450–550 mg/m²); free radical-mediated myocardial damage; alopecia also…

questions.keyPoint: anthracycline antibiotic; acute cardiotoxicity = arrhythmias + heart block immediately post-infusion; chronic cardiotoxicity = dilated cardiomyopathy (cumulative dose-dependent, limit to 450–550 mg/m²); free radical-mediated myocardial damage; alopecia also common

questions.stem: A patient treated with a chemotherapy agent for lung cancer develops pulmonary fibrosis.

choices.label: E
choices.explanation: glycopeptide antibiotic; dose-dependent pulmonary toxicity → progressive pulmonary fibrosis; mechanism = free radical generation + impaired DNA repair in lung (lung lacks bleomycin hydrolase); also methotrexate, busulphan cause pulmonary fibrosis

questions.stem: A 55-year-old man on treatment for prostate cancer complains of gynaecomastia.

choices.label: G
choices.explanation: non-steroidal anti-androgen; blocks dihydrotestosterone receptor → reduces androgen effect on prostate tumour; also blocks peripheral androgen receptors including breast → unopposed oestrogen → gynaecomastia; used to cover GnRH analogue "tumour flare"
